content.page_title: Best Weight Loss Clinics in Toronto (2026)
content.meta_description: Is Ozempic right for your weight loss goals? Our 2026 Canadian review covers clinical effectiveness, cost in CAD, side effects, and prescription access.
content.h1: Best Weight Loss Clinics in Toronto, Ontario (2026)
content.intro: Toronto is home to some of Canada's most advanced medically supervised weight loss programs — from OHIP-funded bariatric centres to telehealth clinics offering same-week GLP-1 prescriptions. Obesity affects 28% of Ontario adults. This guide reviews the best GTA options by clinical approach, cost, and what is covered by OHIP or benefits.
content.local_context: Toronto's weight loss options range from OHIP-covered bariatric surgery (Toronto Western, Humber River) to private clinics like Wharton Medical and telehealth services like Felix. Waitlists for OHIP surgery run 18–24 months; private options offer access in days.

top_picks.name: Wharton Medical Clinic
top_picks.description: Led by Dr. Sean Wharton, this is Canada's most recognized obesity medicine practice. They offer medically supervised weight management combining pharmacotherapy, nutritional counselling, and lifestyle coaching. Multiple GTA locations including North York and Oakville. Known for expert GLP-1 prescribing.
top_picks.best_for: Patients wanting specialist-led obesity medicine with GLP-1 medication management

top_picks.name: Toronto Western Bariatric Network (UHN)
top_picks.tagline: OHIP-covered bariatric surgery for eligible Ontarians
top_picks.description: Part of University Health Network, this flagship program provides OHIP-funded bariatric surgery — gastric bypass and sleeve gastrectomy — for eligible Ontarians with BMI 40+ or 35+ with comorbidities. Pre- and post-operative dietitian and psychology support is included in the program.
top_picks.price_range: Free with OHIP (referral and qualification required)
cons: Strict eligibility: BMI 40+ or 35+ with conditions
top_picks.best_for: Ontarians with severe obesity (BMI 40+) who qualify for OHIP-covered bariatric surgery

top_picks.name: Cleveland Clinic Canada
top_picks.tagline: Premium medical weight management in downtown Toronto
top_picks.description: Cleveland Clinic Canada in Toronto's Financial District offers executive-level medical weight management, including comprehensive metabolic assessments, GLP-1 medication management, and individualized lifestyle programs backed by Cleveland Clinic's global evidence-based protocols. Quick appointments and same-day bloodwork available.
pros: Premium facilities with minimal wait times
cons: Not covered by OHIP or most benefit plans
top_picks.best_for: Executives and professionals wanting a premium, integrated medical weight management experience

top_picks.name: Felix Health (Telehealth)
top_picks.tagline: Online GLP-1 prescriptions for Ontario residents — no waitlist
top_picks.description: Felix is a Canadian telehealth platform connecting Ontario patients with licensed physicians for GLP-1 weight loss medications (Ozempic, Wegovy, Saxenda). Consultations happen online in days, and prescriptions are sent directly to your local pharmacy. No in-person visit required.
cons: GLP-1 medication costs not covered by OHIP
top_picks.best_for: Busy Torontonians who want fast access to GLP-1 medications without a long clinic waitlist

top_picks.name: Medisys Health Group (Toronto)
top_picks.description: Medisys offers medical weight management at their Bay Street clinic, often accessible through corporate health benefits. Programs combine physician oversight, registered dietitian support, and structured lifestyle coaching. Many Torontonians can expense or claim this through their group benefits plan.
pros: Often covered by employer group benefits
cons: Less specialized in obesity medicine than Wharton
top_picks.best_for: Corporate employees with extended health benefits looking for a convenient downtown weight management option

what_to_look_for: Physician oversight — a licensed MD should be directly involved, not just a wellness coach or health educator
Evidence-based protocols — programs should follow Canadian obesity clinical practice guidelines, not fad diets
Medication access — can they prescribe GLP-1s (Ozempic, Wegovy) if you're a candidate?
Transparency on cost — get a full breakdown of consultation fees, medication costs, and what's covered by OHIP or benefits
Long-term follow-up — effective programs include regular check-ins and dose adjustments, not just an initial assessment
content.average_cost_context: Private weight loss clinic fees in Toronto typically range from $150 to $600 per month. Medical consultations average $200–300, while GLP-1 medications (Ozempic, Wegovy) add $250–450/month unless covered by provincial or employer drug plans.
content.insurance_notes: OHIP covers bariatric surgery for eligible Ontarians through the Bariatric Network (BMI 40+ or 35+ with comorbidities, family doctor referral required). Most private clinic fees are not OHIP covered. GLP-1 medications for weight loss are generally not covered by ODB, but many Ontarians have partial coverage through employer extended health plans — check for 'obesity' or 'metabolic' coverage in your benefits booklet.

faqs.question: How do I get a referral to a weight loss clinic in Toronto?
faqs.answer: Ask your family doctor for a referral to the Bariatric Network of Ontario for OHIP-covered surgery, or visit a private clinic like Wharton Medical directly without a referral. Telehealth platforms like Felix require no referral at all.

faqs.question: Does OHIP cover weight loss clinic visits in Toronto?
faqs.answer: OHIP covers bariatric surgery through designated programs like Toronto Western Hospital. Most private weight loss clinic fees and GLP-1 medications are not OHIP covered, but employer drug plans may cover some medications.

faqs.question: Can I get Ozempic for weight loss in Toronto without a long wait?
faqs.answer: Yes — telehealth services like Felix Health can connect you with an Ontario-licensed physician online within days. You pay privately unless your drug plan covers it. Wharton Medical also prescribes GLP-1s with shorter waits than hospital programs.

faqs.question: What is the cheapest weight loss option in Toronto?
faqs.answer: The most affordable is OHIP-covered bariatric surgery through the Bariatric Network — fully funded, but with an 18–24 month waitlist. For faster private care, telehealth options like Felix start at $49 per consultation.

faqs.question: Are Toronto weight loss clinics covered by extended health benefits?
faqs.answer: Many group benefit plans cover registered dietitian consultations ($200–600/year) and physician fees. Some plans also cover GLP-1 medications. Check your benefits booklet under 'paramedical services' and 'prescription drugs.'

faqs.question: What's the difference between a weight loss clinic and seeing my GP?
faqs.answer: Specialized clinics offer structured programs, dietitian support, and clinical expertise in obesity medicine. Your GP can prescribe medications, but dedicated clinics provide ongoing monitoring, dose adjustments, and accountability that improve long-term outcomes.

content.cta_heading: Not sure which Toronto weight loss clinic is right for you?
content.cta_body: Sign up for our free weekly newsletter — we track new clinics, GLP-1 medication availability, and OHIP coverage updates so you always know your options in Ontario.
